title: hieracium subramosum subsp. dentellatum
intro: Hieracium subramosum subsp. dentellatum, also known as the crested hawk's beard, is a flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to help with wound healing and reducing inflammation. The health benefits of Hieracium subramosum subsp. dentellatum include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and act as an antioxidant, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used as a poultice or compress to apply directly to affected areas.
